characters.name: Gemini 2.5 Pro

personality.style: Structured, bullet-pointed analysis with industry context

strengths: Latest public information and industry trends

personality.style: Concrete code examples with detailed debugging steps

characters.name: Claude Haiku 4.5

personality.style: Tool-schema JSON extraction and single-pass web summarize

strengths: Structured JSON via custom tool input_schema
Atomic keyword search queries (legacy Wikipedia pattern)
bestFor: Screening passes with fixed output shape
promptingDos: Define explicit input_schema per extraction task
Wrap source in <content> or <article> tags
Use tool_choice when output shape is fixed
Prefer tool_use over free-form JSON text
When multiple independent reads, expect parallel tool_use in one turn
State output language explicitly; use native script for source text
Escalate high-stakes Japanese synthesis to Sonnet (Haiku JP MMLU ~93.5%)
promptingDonts: Subagent orchestration lead role
Long compaction-heavy sessions
Opus-style JSON prefill (use tools instead)
Split parallel tool_result across separate user messages
Romanized Japanese when kanji/kana source is available

characters.name: Claude Sonnet 4.6

personality.style: Extended thinking before tools; composes compaction, clearing, and memory by workload

promptingDos: Enable extended thinking for multi-step tool plans
Preserve thinking blocks on replay
Add interleaved-thinking-2025-05-14 beta header for tool loops
Use display: omitted when automation does not show thinking text
Batch all parallel tool_result blocks in one user message
Set eager_input_streaming on tools with large payloads (files, long arrays)
Accumulate input_json_delta per block index; parse only at content_block_stop
Set stream: true when using eager_input_streaming or large max_tokens
Prompt for simultaneous independent tool calls when reading multiple sources
Pick compaction vs clearing vs memory by workload
Write durable facts to memory paths for session 2
Author Skills: third-person description, SKILL.md under 500 lines, one-level file refs
Respect context budget warnings; compact or clear before hitting 200k window
Give verification criteria in prompt — tests, build, or linter pass before stop
Run /init for CLAUDE.md starter; keep lean — workflow detail in on-demand skills
On 4.6+ migration: use thinking.type adaptive + effort instead of budget_tokens
Respond in Japanese for user-facing output; idiomatic technical prose
Use native script for non-Latin source material
Fan-out tool workloads: allowed_callers code_execution_20260120 + filter in sandbox
Cache stable system + tool defs: explicit breakpoint or top-level automatic cache_control
Use 1h TTL for batch RAG prefixes; monitor cache_read_input_tokens in usage
Place document block before question text; use logical PDF page numbers in prompts
Place image blocks before question text; label multi-image turns (Image 1:, Image 2:)
Pre-resize screenshots to model limits; request pixel bounding boxes not normalized coords
Use Files API file_id for images in long multi-turn agent threads
Audit third-party Skills before production; API Skills have no network — bundle deps
Use pre-built pptx/xlsx/docx/pdf skill_id on API container for document automation
Commit repo skills under .claude/skills/; use disable-model-invocation for deploy/commit workflows
Use !`cmd` shell injection for live git/PR context in Claude Code skills
Register MCP servers via claude mcp add outside sessions; commit .mcp.json for team project scope
Use --scope user for personal MCP servers across all projects; not Cursor .cursor/mcp.json
Use SessionStart compact hook to re-inject fork rules after Claude Code compaction
PreToolUse hooks to block .env and package-lock edits; PostToolUse for prettier/eslint
Run /context and /doctor when Claude Code config seems ignored; hooks go in settings.json not ~/.claude.json
Use /clear between unrelated tasks; move workflow-specific rules from CLAUDE.md into on-demand skills
Prefer Sonnet for routine coding; Haiku for lightweight subagents; PreToolUse hooks to filter verbose logs
Check plugin Context cost in /plugin Discover before install; run /reload-plugins after enable
Use /loop with dynamic interval for PR babysitting; prefer Monitor tool over fixed poll when possible
Use claude --bare -p in CI scripts; pass --allowedTools explicitly for reproducible runs
Embed claude-cli:// links in internal runbooks; URL-encode q; use repo= for shared clones
Use CLAUDE_CODE_GLOSSARY for term lookup — agentic loop, compaction, hooks vs skills vs commands
Configure autoMode.environment with $defaults for org repos/buckets; use permissions.deny for hard org blocks
Run claude auto-mode config after editing autoMode; never omit $defaults unless you own the full rule list
Enable CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_TELEMETRY + OTEL_LOGS_EXPORTER for fleet audit; keep OTEL_LOG_TOOL_DETAILS off unless SIEM needs command detail
Use Batch API for offline RAG when latency allows — 50% token discount per ANTHROPIC_PRICING
Use adaptive thinking + effort — not manual budget_tokens on Sonnet 4.6
Chunk PDFs exceeding page/context limits; cache document block for repeat Q&A
Preflight large PDF/tool payloads with messages/count_tokens before send
promptingDonts: Expect thinking block after every tool_result (interleaved adds blocks between tools)
Force tool_choice any/name with thinking enabled
Use manual budget_tokens — use adaptive thinking + effort on Sonnet 4.6
Rely on allowed_callers as security boundary for programmatic tools
Mix tool_result with text on programmatic pending turns
Use programmatic calling for 1–2 small sequential tools per turn
Split parallel tool_result across multiple user messages
Parse partial_json before content_block_stop without incremental-UI need
Enable eager_input_streaming on small-schema tools without large payloads
Install untrusted Skills on production systems without file audit
Assume Cursor .cursor/mcp.json applies to Claude Code CLI sessions
Auto-approve all PermissionRequest hooks with empty matcher — scope narrowly
Put hooks or permissions in ~/.claude.json — use ~/.claude/settings.json
Keep bloated CLAUDE.md with workflow-specific detail — use skills for on-demand load
Install untrusted plugins or marketplaces without security review
Leave /loop running past 7 days without recreating — recurring tasks auto-expire
Rely on claude -p without --bare in CI — local hooks/MCP may change behavior
Click external claude-cli:// links without reading Prompt from external link warning
Accumulate failed corrections in one session — /clear and rewrite prompt after two misses
Assume Skills sync between Claude API, claude.ai, and Claude Code
Verbose SKILL.md with basics Claude already knows
Clear irreplaceable tool output
Stack all context primitives without diagnosis
Send password-protected or encrypted PDFs without pre-processing
Ask for normalized 0–1000 bounding boxes on vision tasks
Rely on PDF document-block coordinates for precise overlay mapping
Assume Cursor permission UX maps to Claude Code autoMode settings
Omit $defaults from autoMode.soft_deny or hard_deny — drops built-in security blocks
Put autoMode allow rules in committed .claude/settings.json — classifier ignores shared project settings
Enable OTEL_LOG_RAW_API_BODIES or OTEL_LOG_USER_PROMPTS without backend redaction — exports full conversation
Confuse TechSapo Prometheus (MONITORING_OPERATIONS) with Claude Code OTel — separate pipelines
Treat /usage local dollar estimate as authoritative invoice — use Console or OTel cost metrics
Pin Sonnet 4.5 or Opus 4.1 when same-family latest id is available at equal or lower list price
Place cache_control on per-request timestamps or user text — use static prefix end

characters.name: Claude Opus 4.6

personality.style: Default aggregate tier — synthesizes peer outputs; escalates to 4.8 when gates fail

strengths: Pre-4.7 tokenizer — lower effective $/token on large synthesis inputs
promptingDos: Use as default aggregate model — escalate to 4.8 only when confidence or peer consensus fails gates
Set effort medium for routine synthesis; reserve high for escalation tier
Batch parallel tool_result in one user message on multi-tool aggregate paths
Cache static orchestrator system + tool definitions across aggregate rounds
Preflight aggregate payload with count_tokens before synthesis
promptingDonts: Start on Opus 4.8 when 4.6 aggregate passes quality gates
Use manual budget_tokens — adaptive thinking + effort only
Skip escalation when confidence < 0.7 or peer min < 0.6 after 4.6 pass

characters.name: Claude Opus 4.8

personality.style: Spawns helpers, polls status, collects hub messages, dismisses subagents, one team summary

strengths: JSON prefill and XML-tagged multi-object output
bestFor: Critical taskType synthesis from first pass
Parseable aggregate JSON for downstream consumers
promptingDos: Coordinate via explicit structured messages only
Poll status while helpers run
Collect all reports then kill_subagents
Finish with one consolidated team summary
Enable thinking for hard synthesis; preserve blocks on tool aggregate paths
Use display: omitted for low-latency aggregate when thinking text is not shown
Batch parallel tool_result in one user message on multi-tool aggregate paths
Prefill assistant with { for preamble-free JSON when tools are not used
Use XML wrapper tags for multiple JSON objects
Use claude-opus-4-8 when 4.6 aggregate fails gates or taskType is critical
On 4.8: adaptive thinking + effort only; set effort explicitly (defaults high)
Japanese aggregate synthesis: idiomatic output; preserve English API identifiers
Cache static orchestrator system + tool definitions across aggregate rounds
On Opus 4.8+: mid-session operator policy via role:system in messages (preserves prompt cache)
Preflight aggregate payload size with count_tokens before synthesis rounds
promptingDonts: Assume inline prose reaches other agents
Leave orphaned subagents after synthesis
JSON prefill when chain-of-thought before JSON is required
Force tool_choice with thinking enabled
Default to Opus 4.6 when 4.8 escalation is not required
Put tool output or RAG text in role:system messages
Place mid-conversation system between tool_use and tool_result
